privileged_info_summary: The eraser_transparency@1 task operates on REAL image data sourced from Unsplash's public photo gallery. Two URLs are used (with fallback): photo-1574158622682-e40e69881006 and photo-1551963831-b3b1ca40c98e. Both URLs are accessible and return valid JPEG images. If downloads fail, a synthetic test image is created via ImageMagick. The task uses GIMP to erase portions of the image and verifies transparency creation using PNG format (which correctly preserves alpha channels). The verifier checks: (1) >2% of pixels become transparent, (2) transparency increase ≥2 percentage points from original, (3) <80% of image is erased (content preservation), (4) result is valid PNG format. All verification thresholds and logic are correctly implemented.
